brief_document.title: Active-execution registry and WorkflowHandle
brief_document.purpose: Map a running workflow to its beamr process. The registry is the lookup path for cancel/signal/query and the source for list_workflows, and it keeps a cached status that is always reconciled against the authoritative event projection.
brief_document.task: Implement the registry module: WorkflowHandle (pid, workflow type, loaded version, cached status) and a concurrency-safe registry keyed by (WorkflowId, RunId) supporting insert/lookup/remove/list. Lock poison maps to EngineError::RegistryPoisoned. Provide a reconcile operation that takes the projected WorkflowStatus from aion-core and overwrites the cached status, with the projection winning. Scope is the registry data structure and reconciliation — lifecycle transitions that drive it are later briefs.

requirements.title: WorkflowHandle type
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define WorkflowHandle carrying the beamr pid, the workflow type, the loaded version identifier, and a cached WorkflowStatus.
acceptance: handle.rs defines WorkflowHandle with fields for the beamr pid, the workflow type (the logical entry module name), the loaded version (content hash / version-record identifier from aion-package), and a cached aion_core WorkflowStatus.
WorkflowHandle is Clone and Debug; it stores no beamr runtime state, only the pid value.
registry/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use.

requirements.title: Registry keyed by (WorkflowId, RunId)
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement a concurrency-safe registry mapping (WorkflowId, RunId) to a WorkflowHandle, supporting insert, lookup, remove, and list of live handles.
acceptance: table.rs defines a Registry holding a map keyed by (WorkflowId, RunId) behind a lock (Mutex or RwLock), Default-constructible and Send + Sync.
insert((id, run), handle), get(&id, &run) -> Option<WorkflowHandle>, remove(&id, &run), and list() -> Vec<WorkflowHandle> are provided.
A test inserts two runs of the same WorkflowId and asserts both are retrievable and a stale run does not shadow a newer one.
list() returns a snapshot vector, not a guard, so callers do not hold the lock.

requirements.title: Explicit lock-poison handling
requirements.spec: IF the registry lock is poisoned, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return EngineError::RegistryPoisoned. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT call .unwrap() or .expect() on a lock guard.
acceptance: Every lock acquisition maps a PoisonError to EngineError::RegistryPoisoned (registry methods return Result accordingly).
grep of registry/ shows no .unwrap()/.expect() on lock results.
A test simulates a poisoned lock (e.g. by panicking while holding the guard in a spawned thread) and asserts the next access returns RegistryPoisoned rather than panicking.

requirements.title: Status reconciliation against the event projection
requirements.spec: WHEN reconciling a handle's status, THE SYSTEM SHALL compute the WorkflowStatus by projecting over the workflow's event history using aion-core and SHALL overwrite the cached status with the projection. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT set a handle's status from any source other than that projection.
acceptance: table.rs (or handle.rs) provides reconcile(&id, &run, events: &[Event]) that recomputes status via aion-core's projection and updates the cached status on the handle.
No registry API mutates status with a caller-supplied status value; the only writer is the projection path.
A test builds an event history ending in WorkflowCompleted, reconciles, and asserts the cached status is Completed; a history ending in WorkflowCancelled reconciles to Cancelled.
A test asserts that if the cached status disagrees with the projection, reconcile makes the projection win.

boundaries: SHALL NOT drive lifecycle transitions (start/cancel/complete) — the registry is updated by AE-009/AE-010.
SHALL NOT define WorkflowStatus or its projection — those are aion-core; this brief consumes them.
SHALL NOT query the store or run replay — reconcile takes the events it is given; loading them is AD's append/read path used by lifecycle.
verification: Lock poison maps to EngineError::RegistryPoisoned with no panic (CO9).
Status is only ever set from the aion-core projection (CO10).

checklist.text: WorkflowHandle carries the beamr pid, the workflow type, the loaded version, and a cached WorkflowStatus.
The registry maps (WorkflowId, RunId) to a WorkflowHandle and supports insert, lookup, remove, and list of live handles.
The registry is concurrency-safe and lock poison is mapped to EngineError::RegistryPoisoned rather than panicking.
The cached status is reconciled against aion-core's event-history projection and the projection wins on disagreement; no code path sets status independently of events.

stories.text: As a workflow caller, I want to start a workflow by type and get a handle so that I can later await its result or cancel it by ID.
As a workflow caller, I want to list and query workflows by type, status, and time so that I can see what is running and what has finished without loading full histories.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a runtime gate it at runtime, never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion depends only on aion-core, aion-store, aion-package, and beamr among workspace crates — no aion-server, no store backend crate, no networking crate. Structural; must hold.
Transport-agnostic: no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket dependency or code anywhere in the crate.
Only the runtime module imports beamr; every other module uses the RuntimeHandle (per D1).
All library errors are thiserror EngineError variants; no anyhow in this library crate. No .unwrap()/.expect() outside tests.
Mutex/RwLock poison handled explicitly and mapped to a typed EngineError; no .unwrap() on a lock guard.
WorkflowStatus is read from aion-core's event projection; the registry's cached status is never the source of truth (per aion-core CO7).
aion hardcodes no scheduler thread count, no timeout, and no retry default; configurable values come from the builder or are deferred to beamr's own default.
Module namespacing uses aion-package's transform unchanged; the engine never re-derives namespaced names (per D8).
Engine tests run against InMemoryStore; no test requires a database (per aion-core D8).

resolved_context.intention: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows, runs each as a BEAM process on an embedded beamr runtime, supervises them through a three-level tree, dispatches their activities as linked child processes, and cancels or reports on them by ID. Starting a workflow is spawning a process; cancelling it is killing that process, which through links kills its activity children too; an activity crash propagates an exit signal the trapping workflow process receives as a message. The crate is transport-agnostic — no HTTP/gRPC/WebSocket — keeping the embedded path free of network dependencies. This cluster owns lifecycle, process management, supervision, and module loading; it defers durability/replay (AD) and time/signals/queries/children/concurrency (AT) to their owners, surfacing their entry points on Engine and stating the seams explicitly.

provenance.quote: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows,
